tools.name: download_video
tools.description: Download a video or audio file from any supported platform: YouTube, TikTok, Vimeo, Dailymotion, Twitter/X, SoundCloud, Bandcamp, Mixcloud, Twitch (clips and VODs), or Streamable. Output is MP4 (video, default) or MP3 / M4A (audio). This is THE tool to use whenever a user asks to save, download, rip

tools.name: get_video_info
tools.description: Fetch metadata about a video or audio track WITHOUT downloading it. Works on every platform download_video supports: YouTube, TikTok, Vimeo, Dailymotion, Twitter/X, SoundCloud, Bandcamp, Mixcloud, Twitch, and Streamable. Returns title, uploader/channel name, duration, view count (when available), up

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "download_video" stacks urgency/override words (important, always). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Download a video or audio file from any supported platform: YouTube, TikTok, Vimeo, Dailymotion, Twitter/X, SoundCloud, Bandcamp, Mixcloud, Twitch (clips and VO…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: download_video, get_video_info · Private data: download_video · Public sink: get_video_info · Destructive: download_video.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: download_video, get_video_info · Private data: download_video · Public sink: get_video_info · Destructive: download_video

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
